new_classes.label_en: liqueur or cordial in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 70 90)
new_classes.label_de: Likör oder Cordial in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2208 70 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Liqueur or cordial of CN heading 2208 70 presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres, as distinguished from CN 2208 70 10 which covers containers of 2 litres or less. Liqueurs and cordials are spirituous beverages with added sugar or other sweetening matter and flavouring substances; see HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, first paragraph (B) and third paragraph.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criterion for CN 2208 70 90 vs. 2208 70 10 is container volume (> 2 L vs. ≤ 2 L), which is captured via the BFO part-whole relation to eucn:LargeContainer. The product characteristics shared by both subheadings — sweetening matter and flavouring substance — are also covered via hasAddedSweetener and hasAddedFlavouring restrictions. Disjointness with the sibling small-container class (22087010) is expressed. A minor gap remains: the HS Explanatory Note references a minimum sugar content threshold for liqueurs (typically 100 g/litre for standard liqueurs, higher for certain types), which would require a numeric quality axiom on eucn:SweeteningQuality; this minimum sugar content is referenced in the legal note but not precisely specified here, and is therefore not fully formalised in the current axioms, reducing coverage slightly. Additionally, the note refers to the third paragraph of the HS EN to 2208, which specifies that 'cordials' are included — this is handled at the parent eucn:Liqueur class level rather than requiring a new class.
